241:   dm::dmi_req_t  debug_req;

244: `ifdef RISCV_FESVR_SIM
245: 
246:   initial begin
247:     $display("[INFO] instantiating FESVR DTM in simulation.");
248:   end
249: 
250:   // SiFive's SimDTM Module
251:   // Converts to DPI calls
252:   logic [31:0] sim_exit; // TODO: wire this up in the testbench
253:   logic [1:0] debug_req_bits_op;
254:   assign dmi_req.op = dm::dtm_op_t'(debug_req_bits_op);
255: 
256:   SimDTM i_SimDTM (
257:     .clk                  ( clk_i                ),
258:     .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
259:     .debug_req_valid      ( debug_req_valid      ),
260:     .debug_req_ready      ( debug_req_ready      ),
261:     .debug_req_bits_addr  ( debug_req.addr       ),
262:     .debug_req_bits_op    ( debug_req_bits_op    ),
263:     .debug_req_bits_data  ( debug_req.data       ),
264:     .debug_resp_valid     ( debug_resp_valid     ),
265:     .debug_resp_ready     ( debug_resp_ready     ),
266:     .debug_resp_bits_resp ( debug_resp.resp      ),
267:     .debug_resp_bits_data ( debug_resp.data      ),
268:     .exit                 ( sim_exit             )
269:   );
270: 
271: `else // RISCV_FESVR_SIM
272: 
273:   logic        tck, tms, trst_n, tdi, tdo, tdo_oe;
274: 
275:   dmi_jtag i_dmi_jtag (
276:     .clk_i                                ,
277:     .rst_ni                               ,
278:     .testmode_i                           ,
279:     .we_flag          ( we_flag_4        ),
280:     .jtag_hash_i      ( jtag_hash        ),
281:     .okey_hash_i      ( okey_hash        ),
282:     .ikey_hash_i      ( ikey_hash        ),
283:     .jtag_unlock_o    ( jtag_unlock      ),
284:     .dmi_req_o        ( debug_req        ),
285:     .dmi_req_valid_o  ( debug_req_valid  ),
286:     .dmi_req_ready_i  ( debug_req_ready  ),
287:     .dmi_resp_i       ( debug_resp       ),
288:     .dmi_resp_ready_o ( debug_resp_ready ),
289:     .dmi_resp_valid_i ( debug_resp_valid ),
290:     .dmi_rst_no       (                  ), // not connected
291:     .tck_i            ( tck              ),
292:     .tms_i            ( tms              ),
293:     .trst_ni          ( trst_n           ),
294:     .td_i             ( tdi              ),
295:     .td_o             ( tdo              ),
296:     .tdo_oe_o         ( tdo_oe           )
297:   );
298: 
299: `ifdef RISCV_JTAG_SIM
300: 
301:   initial begin
302:     $display("[INFO] instantiating JTAG DTM in simulation.");
303:   end
304: 
305:   // SiFive's SimJTAG Module
306:   // Converts to DPI calls
307:   logic [31:0] sim_exit; // TODO: wire this up in the testbench
308:   SimJTAG i_SimJTAG (
309:     .clock                ( clk_i                ),
310:     .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
311:     .enable               ( jtag_enable[0]       ),
312:     .init_done            ( init_done            ),
313:     .jtag_TCK             ( tck                  ),
314:     .jtag_TMS             ( tms                  ),
315:     .jtag_TDI             ( trst_n               ),
316:     .jtag_TRSTn           ( td                   ),
317:     .jtag_TDO_data        ( td                   ),
318:     .jtag_TDO_driven      ( tdo_oe               ),
319:     .exit                 ( sim_exit             )
320:   );
321: 
322:   assign td_o     = 1'b0  ;
323:   assign tdo_oe_o = 1'b0  ;
324: 
325: `else // RISCV_JTAG_SIM
326: 
327:   assign tck      = tck_i   ;
328:   assign tms      = tms_i   ;
329:   assign trst_n   = trst_ni ;
330:   assign tdi      = td_i    ;
331:   assign td_o     = tdo     ;
332:   assign tdo_oe_o = tdo_oe  ;
333: 
334: `endif // RISCV_JTAG_SIM
335: `endif // RISCV_FESVR_SIM

1: // Description: Access Control registers
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: 
6: module reglk_wrapper #(
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
9:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
10:     parameter NB_SLAVE = 1,
11:     parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14
12: )(
13:            clk_i,
14:            rst_ni,
15:            jtag_unlock,
16:            reglk_ctrl_i,
17:            acct_ctrl_i,
18:            reglk_ctrl_o,
19:            axi_req_i, 
20:            axi_resp_o,
21:            rst_9
22:        );
23: 
24: 
25:     input  logic                   clk_i;
26:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
27:     input  logic                   jtag_unlock;
28:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock values
29:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
30:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
31:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
32:     output logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   reglk_ctrl_o; // register lock values
33:     input logic rst_9;
34: 
35: 
36: // internal signals
37: 
38: logic [15:0] reglk_ctrl ;  
39: reg [5:0][31:0] reglk_mem ;   // this size is sufficient for 24 slaves where each slave gets 8 bits
40: 
41: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
42: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
43: logic                      en, en_acct;
44: logic                      we;
45: logic [63:0] wdata;
46: logic [63:0] rdata;
47: 
48: assign reglk_ctrl_o = {reglk_mem[5], reglk_mem[4], reglk_mem[3], reglk_mem[2], reglk_mem[1], reglk_mem[0]}; 
49: 
50: assign reglk_ctrl = reglk_ctrl_i;
51: 
52: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
53:     // -----------------------------
54:     // AXI Interface Logic
55:     // -----------------------------
56:     axi_lite_interface #(
57:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
58:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
59:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
60:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
61:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
62:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
63:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
64:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
65:         .address_o  ( address    ),
66:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
67:         .we_o       ( we         ),
68:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
69:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
70:     );
71: 
72:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
73: 
74: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
75: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
76: // Write side
77: integer j;
78: always @(posedge clk_i)
79:     begin
80:         if(~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9))
81:             begin
82:               for (j=0; j < 6; j=j+1) begin
83:                 reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0;
84:               end
85:             end
86:         else if(en && we)
87:             case(address[7:3])
88:                 0:
89:                     reglk_mem[0]  <= reglk_ctrl[3] ? reglk_mem[0] : wdata;
90:                 1:
91:                     reglk_mem[1]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[1] : wdata; 
92:                 2:
93:                     reglk_mem[2]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[3] : wdata;
94:                 3:
95:                     reglk_mem[3]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[3] : wdata;
96:                 4:
97:                     reglk_mem[4]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[4] : wdata;
98:                 5:
99:                     reglk_mem[5]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[5] : wdata;
100:                 default:
101:                     ;
102:             endcase
103:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
104: 
105: //// Read side
106: always @(*)
107:     begin
108:       rdata = 64'b0; 
109:       if (en) begin
110:         case(address[7:3])
111:             0:
112:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl[0] ? 'b0 : reglk_mem[0]; 
113:             1:          
114:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl[0] ? 'b0 : reglk_mem[1];
115:             2:          
116:
[TRUNCATED]

1: // Description: Access Control registers
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: module acct_wrapper #(
6:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
9:     parameter NB_SLAVE = 1,
10:     parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 9
11: )(
12:            clk_i,
13:            rst_ni,
14:            reglk_ctrl_i,
15:            acct_ctrl_i,
16:            acc_ctrl_o,
17:            we_flag,
18:            axi_req_i, 
19:            axi_resp_o,
20:            rst_6
21:        );
22: 
23: 
24:     input  logic                   clk_i;
25:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
26:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock valuesA
27:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
28:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
29:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
30:     output logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl_o; // Access control values
31:     input logic  we_flag;
32: 
33:     localparam AcCt_MEM_SIZE = NB_SLAVE*3 ;  // 32*3 bytes of access control for each slave interface
34: 
35:     input rst_6;
36: 
37: // internal signals
38: 
39: reg [AcCt_MEM_SIZE-1:0][31:0] acct_mem ; 
40: logic [15:0] reglk_ctrl ;  
41: 
42: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
43: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
44: logic                      en, en_acct;
45: logic                      we;
46: logic [63:0] wdata;
47: logic [63:0] rdata;
48: 
49: assign acc_ctrl_o = {acct_mem[3*0+2], acct_mem[3*0+1], acct_mem[3*0+0]|{8{we_flag}}}; 
50: 
51: assign reglk_ctrl = reglk_ctrl_i;
52: 
53: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
54:     // -----------------------------
55:     // AXI Interface Logic
56:     // -----------------------------
57:     axi_lite_interface #(
58:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
59:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
60:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
61:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
62:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
63:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
64:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
65:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
66:         .address_o  ( address    ),
67:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
68:         .we_o       ( we         ),
69:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
70:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
71:     );
72: 
73:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
74: 
75: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
76: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
77: // Write side
78: integer j;
79: always @(posedge clk_i)
80:     begin
81:         if(~(rst_ni && ~rst_6))
82:             begin
83:               for (j=0; j < AcCt_MEM_SIZE; j=j+1) begin
84:                 acct_mem[j] <= 32'hffffffff; 
85:               end
86:             end
87:         else if(en && we)
88:             case(address[10:3])
89:                 0:
90:                     acct_mem[00]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[00] : wdata;
91:                 1:                                             
92:                     acct_mem[01]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[01] : wdata; 
93:                 2:                                             
94:                     acct_mem[02]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[02] : wdata;
95:                 3:                                              
96:                     acct_mem[03]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[03] : wdata;
97:                 4:                                               
98:                     acct_mem[04]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[04] : wdata;
99:                 5:                                               
100:                     acct_mem[05]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[05] : wdata;
101:                 6:                                              
102:                     acct_mem[06]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? acct_mem[06] : wdata;
103:                 7:                                              
104:                     acct_mem
[TRUNCATED]

160: // Implement MD5 I/O memory map interface
161: // Read side
162: //always @(~write)
163: always @(*)
164:     begin
165:       rdata = 64'b0; 
166:       if (en) begin
167:         rdata = key0[address[8:3]]; 
168:         case(address[8:3])
169:             0:
170:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[0] ? 'b0 : {31'b0, start};
171:             1:
172:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[2] ? 'b0 : p_c[3];
173:             2:
174:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[2] ? 'b0 : p_c[2];
175:             3:
176:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[2] ? 'b0 : p_c[1];
177:             4:
178:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[2] ? 'b0 : p_c[0];
179:             11:
180:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[6] ? 'b0 : {31'b0, ct_valid};
181:             12:
182:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : ct[31:0];
183:             13:                                                 
184:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : ct[63:32];
185:             14:                                                 
186:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : ct[95:64];
187:             15:                                                 
188:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : ct[127:96];
189:             default:
190:                 if (ct_valid)
191:                     rdata = 32'b0;
192:         endcase
193:       end // if
194:     end // always @ (*)
195: 
196: 
197: // select the proper key
198: 
199: assign key_big = key_sel[1] ? key_big2 : ( key_sel[0] ? key_big1 : key_big0 );  
200: 
201: 
202: aes_192_sed aes(
203:             .clk(clk_i),
204:             .state(state_big),
205:             .p_c_text(p_c_big),
206:             .key(key_big),
207:             .start(start),
208:             .out(ct),
209:             .out_valid(ct_valid)
210:         );
211: 
212: endmodule

186: 
187: // select the proper key
188: 
189: assign cii_K = key_sel[1] ? cii_K2 : ( key_sel[0] ? cii_K1 : cii_K0 );
190: 
191: aes2_interface aes2_interface(
192:     .clk(clk_i),
193:     .iv(state_big),
194:     .rst(rst_ni && ~rst_11),
195:     .start(start),
196:     .cii_K(cii_K),
197:     .input_pc(dii_data),
198:     
199:     .Out_data_final(ct),
200:     .ct_valid_out(ct_valid)
201:     );
202: 
203: endmodule

{
  "scope_id": "piton_ariane_riscv_peripherals_permission_scope",
  "module2_inspection_results": [
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-RISCVPERIPH-DBG-001",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "external JTAG requester via tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i, SimJTAG, or SimDTM",
      "operation": "issue DMI requests that can trigger debug requests, ndmreset/dmactive effects, and debug-module slave/master memory transactions",
      "object": "dm_top controls: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave_* and dm_master_*",
      "expected_guard": "DMI/JTAG requests should be gated by authenticated debug authorization such as jtag_unlock/debug enable before reaching dm_top capabilities.",
      "observed_behavior": "The real JTAG path connects dmi_jtag DMI outputs directly to debug_req/debug_req_valid, and dm_top consumes debug_req_valid/debug_req without an explicit jtag_unlock gate at the top-level dm_top boundary. SimDTM similarly drives debug_req_valid/debug_req directly under RISCV_FESVR_SIM. jtag_unlock is produced by dmi_jtag but is not used to gate dm_top DMI inputs in riscv_peripherals.",
      "path": "tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i -> dmi_jtag -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/dm_master/dm_slave; SimDTM -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top in simulation build",
      "state_condition": "No top-level condition on testmode_i, debug_mode_i, or jtag_unlock is visible on dm_top DMI request inputs; dmi_rst_ni is rst_ni. dmi_jtag receives testmode_i and hash inputs, but top-level does not enforce jtag_unlock before dm_top.",
      "impact": "An unauthenticated or insufficiently authenticated JTAG/DTM requester could drive the debug module interface, potentially forcing hart debug entry, non-debug-module reset, or debug memory transactions through dm_master/dm_slave, compromising system control and memory confidentiality/integrity.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:275-287: dmi_jtag connects .jtag_unlock_o(jtag_unlock), .dmi_req_o(debug_req), .dmi_req_valid_o(debug_req_valid), .dmi_req_ready_i(debug_req_ready), .dmi_resp_i(debug_resp).",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:327-332: external JTAG pins tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i are assigned to internal JTAG signals.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:353-388: dm_top exports ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o and consumes .dmi_req_valid_i(debug_req_valid), .dmi_req_i(debug_req), without visible jtag_unlock gating.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:256-268: SimDTM drives debug_req_valid and debug_req fields directly in RISCV_FESVR_SIM."
      ]
    },
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-REGLK-JTAG-002",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "JTAG/debug requester that can cause jtag_unlock",
      "operation": "clear/unlock register-lock control state",
      "object": "reglk_ctrl_o derived from reglk_mem in reglk_wrapper",
      "expected_guard": "jtag_unlock should only clear/override lock state under authenticated and authorized lifecycle/debug state.",
      "observed_behavior": "reglk_wrapper treats jtag_unlock as part of the reset condition. When jtag_unlock is asserted, all reglk_mem entries are cleared to zero, which deasserts lock controls. The wrapper itself does not qualify jtag_unlock with any lifecycle/debug authorization; the top-level simply connects jtag_unlock from dmi_jtag to reglk_wrapper.",
      "path": "JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag.jtag_unlock_o -> riscv_peripherals.jtag_unlock -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_mem clear -> reglk_ctrl_o",
      "state_condition": "On any clock edge where !(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9) is true, including jtag_unlock=1, reglk_mem[0..5] <= 0. This is a level-sensitive clear behavior sampled by clk_i.",
      "impact": "If jtag_unlock can be asserted by an attacker or by insufficiently protected debug access, all downstream register-lock protections can be cleared, reopening writes/reads to crypto, access-control, reset, and other protected peripherals.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:275-284: dmi_jtag produces .jtag_unlock_o(jtag_unlock).",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:1814-1823: reglk_wrapper receives .jtag_unlock(jtag_unlock) and outputs .reglk_ctrl_o(reglk_ctrl).",
        "reglk_wrapper.sv:78-84: if (~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9)) all reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0.",
        "reglk_wrapper.sv:48: reglk_ctrl_o is concatenation of reglk_mem entries."
      ]
    },
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-AES-WRITELOCK-003",
      "status": "obligation_satisfied",
      "subject": "software or NoC/AXI bus master accessing AES0/AES1/AES2 MMIO",
      "operation": "write AES start/control/plaintext/state/key registers",
      "object": "AES start/control, p_c/block/state, key0/key1/key2/key_reg*, key_sel/keylen registers",
      "expected_guard": "Writes to protected AES registers should be blocked when reglk_ctrl_i bits indicate locked and should also be subject to access-control enable.",
      "observed_behavior": "All three AES wrappers derive en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. Write updates occur only under en && we. Individual register updates retain the old value when the relevant reglk_ctrl_i bit is asserted. debug_mode_i masks selected core key inputs but does not create an alternate write path.",
      "path": "NoC AES port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> aes*_axi_req -> AES wrapper axi_lite_interface -> en/we/address decode -> AES registers",
      "state_condition": "When acct_ctrl_i=0, en=0 and writes are ignored. When reglk_ctrl_i bit for a field is 1, assignment keeps existing value. Per-block resets rst_1/rst_2/rst_11 clear AES state/key registers where implemented.",
      "impact": "Write-lock enforcement reduces unauthorized modification of AES keys/control after policy lock; no unguarded AES write path was observed in inspected wrappers.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "aes0_wrapper.sv:78: en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i.",
        "aes0_wrapper.sv:96-153: start/p_c/key/state/key_sel updates are under en && we and guarded by reglk_ctrl_i[1/3/5/7].",
        "aes1_wrapper.sv:201: en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i.",
        "aes1_wrapper.sv:241-266: control/block/key writes retain old values when corresponding reglk_ctrl_i bits are set.",
        "aes2_wrapper.sv:79: en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i.",
        "aes2_wrapper.sv:99-144: start/p_c/state/key/key_sel writes are guarded by reglk_ctrl_i bits.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:1191-1199, 1277-1285: AES wrappers receive reglk_ctrl slices and acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i] bits."
      ]
    },

      "obligation_id": "PERM-AES-READ-004",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "software or NoC/AXI bus master reading AES MMIO",
      "operation": "read AES key/internal state through rdata",
      "object": "AES key material, especially aes0 key0 array; AES result/state read data",
      "expected_guard": "Secret AES key/internal state should not be readable, or should only be readable through explicit lock/authorization checks.",
      "observed_behavior": "AES0 read logic assigns rdata = key0[address[8:3]] before the case statement. Key address cases for key0/key1/key2 are not explicitly overridden, so reads to key0-mapped addresses can return key0 words without a reglk_ctrl_i read guard. AES1 key reads are guarded by reglk_ctrl_i[2]. AES2 has a similar default rdata = key0[address[8:3]], though its key index/address mapping appears inconsistent and may yield invalid/out-of-range data for some addresses.",
      "path": "NoC AES0 port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> aes0_axi_req -> aes0_wrapper read mux -> rdata -> axi_resp_o -> NoC response",

      "impact": "AES secret key material may be exposed over MMIO/NoC, enabling software or
[TRUNCATED]
